content.problemStatement:
Bugs Bunny is very smart, but he keeps constantly worrying about the future and is always anxious. Therefore, he keeps hopping between towns without standing still.

The place where Bugs Bunny lives has NNN towns (numbered 111 through NNN) with one-way roads connecting them in such a way that they form an arborescence (a tree with all paths directed from the root), rooted at town 111. Therefore, each town except the root has exactly one incoming road; for each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí11 \le i \le N-11‚â§i‚â§N‚àí1), there is a road from town PiP_iPi‚Äã to town i+1i+1i+1. Each town has a distinct rating associated with it; for each valid iii, the iii-th town has rating AiA_iAi‚Äã.

From a town iii, Bugs Bunny is allowed to hop to town jjj (without visiting any other towns in between), if the following conditions are satisfied:


there is a path between towns iii and jjj, i.e. a path from town iii to town jjj or from town jjj to town iii
town jjj has a lower rating than town iii, i.e. Aj<AiA_j \lt A_iAj‚Äã<Ai‚Äã
This way, Bugs Bunny can visit a sequence of towns by hopping, starting anywhere and stopping whenever he chooses. Clearly, he cannot keep hopping forever, so the number of such sequences is finite.

Tell Bugs Bunny the number of sequences of two or more towns which can be formed by hopping, given that he can start at any town and stop at any town. Since this number can be very large, calculate it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN‚àí1P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_{N-1}P1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚àí1‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer --- the number of valid sequences of visited towns modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã are pairwise distinct
the graph described on the input is a directed tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105
**Subtask #1 (20 points):** the tree is a straight line --- there is a single path starting at town 111 and passing through all towns
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The possible hops for Bugs Bunny are from town 111 to town 333 and from town 222 to town 111. Therefore, the 333 possible sequences of visited towns are (2,1,3)(2,1,3)(2,1,3), (2,1)(2,1)(2,1) and (1,3)(1,3)(1,3).
